title: Chain: _safe_float TypeError/ValueError swallow (silent-failure-hunter HIGH #1) defeats EVERY new WARNING in metrics.py for the 'returned value bad type' failure mode
summary: Chains silent-failure-hunter HIGH conf-8 (#1) with the sweep's load-bearing thesis. The new WARNING fires ONLY when qs.stats.X RAISES — but qs.stats functions are notoriously inconsistent about whether they raise vs return NaN vs return a numpy array vs return a complex scalar on edge data. For the common edge case (returns series with all-zero variance — common for tiny strategies), qs.stats.smart_sortino returns NaN (not raise), `_safe_float(NaN)` returns None silently, and NO WARNING fires. Pre-sweep this was the SAME behavior. Post-sweep the operator is led to believe 'no WARNING == no failure' — but smart_sortino IS broken for this strategy. The fail-loud transformation is therefore incomplete: it catches the RAISE failure mode but not the NaN failure mode, and the sweep's WARNING-naming-the-scalar pattern teaches operators to triage by absence-of-warning. Subtle false-negative. STATUS: APPLIED — _safe_float now logs DEBUG on both the TypeError/ValueError path and the NaN/Inf coerce path, providing the operator-triage trail without WARNING spam.
evidence: qs.stats source: many functions return np.nan on insufficient data instead of raising. `_safe_float(np.nan)` returns None (line 176 `if math.isnan(f): return None`). The new sweep WARNING is never triggered. Operator sees `metrics_json['smart_sortino']` missing in the JSON with no log — same surface the sweep was meant to eliminate.
fix: Either (a) inside each try block, after the assignment, check `if metrics_json[k] is None: logger.debug(...)`; or (b) inside _safe_float, log at DEBUG when math.isnan(f) is true. Option (b) is the surgical fix and addresses the broader 'silent NaN coerce' surface. APPLIED (option b).

title: The 12 new tests share `caplog.at_level(logging.WARNING, logger='quantalyze.analytics.metrics')` — pytest's caplog handler scoping may MISS warnings emitted from sub-loggers in CI's parallelized runner
summary: pytest's caplog by default attaches a handler to the ROOT logger and a level filter at the specified logger. The new tests scope `caplog.at_level(logging.WARNING, logger='quantalyze.analytics.metrics')`. metrics.py uses `logger = logging.getLogger('quantalyze.analytics.metrics')` so warnings ARE on that logger. BUT: in CI's parallelized pytest-xdist runner, logger propagation can be disabled or filtered by other tests that ran in the same worker. If a prior test in the same worker called `logging.getLogger('quantalyze').propagate = False` or attached a custom handler, the caplog WARNING capture may not see the records. Test passes locally (single-worker) but flakes in CI. STATUS: DEFERRED — not changed because PR CI is currently green. Will be addressed if/when xdist flakiness materializes. Documenting the design intent (use exact logger name) as a comment.
evidence: pytest-xdist + caplog interaction documented in pytest issue trackers. The test_metrics.py suite uses `logger='quantalyze.analytics.metrics'` — exact-name match. If propagate is disabled OR the logger was re-instantiated by another test's import-side-effect, caplog misses records.
fix: Defensive: change `caplog.at_level(logging.WARNING, logger='quantalyze.analytics.metrics')` to ALSO call `logging.getLogger('quantalyze.analytics.metrics').propagate = True` inside each test (or as a session-scoped fixture). DEFERRED.

title: `compute_all_metrics_outlier_ratios_failure_logs_warning` is a strict tautology — passes on a complete bare-pass revert of the outlier block
summary: Per pr-test MED #3: the test never calls compute_all_metrics. It raises RuntimeError IN THE TEST, emits the WARNING IN THE TEST, then asserts the source contains 'outlier ratios failed'. If a /simplify pass reverted metrics.py:537-541 to `except Exception: pass` BUT kept the comment line `# outlier ratios failed (note: this comment is now stale)` somewhere in the file, the test passes. The test ENCODES the comment-string contract, not the behavior contract. STATUS: APPLIED — test rewritten to monkeypatch pd.Series.mean with a dtype='bool' guard, which uniquely targets the outlier block (boolean Series .mean() is exclusive to this site). Tightened assertion to require the exact log prefix 'outlier ratios failed' AND exc_info=True propagation.
evidence: test_metrics.py:1029-1077: `inspect.getsource(metrics_module)` grep for 'outlier ratios failed'. The string can appear anywhere — comment, docstring, dead code path.
fix: Replace the test with a real trigger: pass a returns Series engineered to break the inner block (e.g., a Series of all NaN where `returns.mean()` returns NaN and `std()` returns NaN — but NaN > 0 is False, so the if-branch is skipped and no WARNING fires — that's not a trigger either). The genuine trigger is harder than it looks because the inner block has a `if std_ret > 0` guard. To force the try block to RAISE, monkeypatch `returns.mean` to raise — then compute_all_metrics catches the raise at the outlier site and emits the WARNING. APPLIED (using bool-Series.mean monkeypatch).

title: Bybit branch comment block (L578-L595) is 17 lines of explanation that pushes the function past readable line-count — and the 'best-effort' framing licenses future engineers to add MORE swallows
summary: The new comment explains the pre-sweep failure modes (a) RPC raised and (b) ISO conversion raised. Now any reader sees: 'Both are now logged at WARNING. We deliberately keep the call best-effort (no re-raise) because fetch_daily_pnl is a fire-and-forget enrichment path.' The 'best-effort enrichment' framing — combined with the sweep's other 'best-effort' comments — establishes a precedent that 'best-effort' is a license to log-then-continue without escalation. A future engineer reading this comment may copy the pattern to a NEW silent-failure site without considering whether THAT site is truly fire-and-forget. The sweep is establishing fail-loud-with-license-to-stay-silent as the dominant pattern — which is a subtle anti-pattern. STATUS: DEFERRED — design-intent note captured in .review/follow-ups.md F-5.
evidence: exchange.py:574-595 — 17 lines. Combined with 7 'fail-soft' comments in metrics.py, the codebase now teaches 'silent-with-log' as the default escape hatch. Real fail-loud means RAISE upward.
fix: This is a meta-finding about review culture, not a code defect. Recommendation: in the FIX-REPORT, add a 'design intent' note that 'best-effort' applies ONLY to existing fire-and-forget paths. DEFERRED.

title: Chain: outer wrapper at L620 swallows everything — combined with inner WARNING-only, the parent sync gets ZERO ERROR signal for partial Bybit data
summary: Chains silent-failure-hunter MED conf-8 (#2). The outer `except Exception as e: logger.error('fetch_daily_pnl failed')` catches everything that escapes. Pre-sweep: Bybit inner failure → silently swallowed by inner pass → outer never fires → no log at all. Post-sweep: Bybit inner failure → WARNING + return [] → outer never fires (no escape). Bybit/Binance fail = WARNING; OKX fail = ERROR. Operator-dashboard alerting that pages on ERROR but not WARNING would silently ignore Bybit outages. STATUS: DEFERRED — would require touching OKX branch which is out of PR scope. Documented in .review/follow-ups.md F-2.
evidence: exchange.py:404-619 (Bybit and Binance branches each have their own try). exchange.py:620 (outer except catches only what escapes the per-branch tries). OKX (L405-L521) has NO outer try inside the branch — its failure escapes to the L620 handler.
fix: Either (a) wrap OKX in its own try-with-WARNING for consistency; or (b) escalate Bybit/Binance to ERROR. Option (a) is the surgical fix. DEFERRED.

title: caplog.at_level(logger='quantalyze.analytics.metrics') in new tests uses the precise logger name — a rename of the logger to 'quantalyze.metrics' would silently break ALL 11 tests with no error
summary: Both files declare loggers via hard-coded name strings: metrics.py:11 `logger = logging.getLogger('quantalyze.analytics.metrics')` and exchange.py:10 `logger = logging.getLogger('quantalyze.analytics')`. The new tests use `caplog.at_level(logger='quantalyze.analytics.metrics')` — a string match. STATUS: DEFERRED — the broken-on-logger-rename behavior is the DESIRED behavior (rename detection). Dropping `logger=` kwarg is a separate refactor.
evidence: test_metrics.py:988, 1018, 1062. test_exchange.py:2820, 2876. All hard-code logger names.
fix: Drop the `logger=` kwarg in caplog.at_level. caplog defaults to capturing at the level for the root logger, which sees all child loggers via propagation. DEFERRED.

title: Chain: pr-test MED #3 (outlier tautology) + red-team HIGH #3 (same site) — combined the outlier-ratios behavior is unverified despite a test existing
summary: Both pr-test-analyzer and red-team independently flagged the outlier-ratios test as tautological. Combined severity: the outlier-ratios fail-loud transformation has effectively ZERO regression coverage. STATUS: APPLIED — see HIGH #3 above (same fix).
evidence: Combined evidence from pr-test MED #3 and red-team HIGH #3.
fix: Same as both: real-trigger rewrite. APPLIED.

title: 3 untested sweep sites (var_1m_99, skewness, kurtosis) — each is a load-bearing WARNING that a /simplify pass would silently remove
summary: Per pr-test HIGH #1: 3 sites have no test. Adversarial: a /simplify pass that 'reduces comment noise' (red-team MED #4) could collapse the now-verbose try-except back to bare-pass under the guise of 'these aren't tested anyway, can't be load-bearing'. The /simplify rule is 'verify load-bearing via tests' — and these 3 sites would FAIL that check. STATUS: APPLIED — 3 dedicated tests added (test_compute_all_metrics_var_1m_99_np_percentile_failure_logs_warning, test_compute_all_metrics_skewness_pandas_failure_logs_warning, test_compute_all_metrics_kurtosis_pandas_failure_logs_warning).
evidence: 3 sites without test, 11+1 lines of WARNING-emitter code per site = ~36 LoC of /simplify-vulnerable code.

title: Challenge — code-reviewer LOW #4 (per-scalar `# fail-soft` comments are noise) is WRONG: the per-scalar comments PROTECT against /simplify collapsing them
summary: code-reviewer LOW #4 flags the 11 'fail-soft: optional scalar' single-line comments as redundant noise. Counter-argument: a future /simplify pass that runs file-by-file (not change-by-change) operates on local context. Without the per-scalar comment, /simplify sees `try: metrics_json[k] = X; except Exception as exc: logger.warning(...)` and may decide 'this is the most verbose pattern in the file, can collapse to inline'. With the comment, /simplify sees the intent annotation and skips. The 11 comments are a DEFENSE against future bare-pass reversion. STATUS: APPLIED (challenge wins) — code-reviewer LOW #4 downgraded to 'keep comments as anti-/simplify defense'. No code change.
evidence: /simplify pattern: localized refactor. Block-level docstrings get missed when collapsing local patterns. The 11 comments cost 11 lines; the protection cost is bounded.
fix: DOWNGRADE code-reviewer LOW #4 from 'remove the comments' to 'keep'. No code change. APPLIED.

title: `returns_len_for_log` is computed BEFORE NaN-row filtering — log claims length that may differ from the qs.stats input length
summary: At line 324, `returns_len_for_log = len(returns)`. But the function earlier (L233-L249) logs about NaN day filtering BUT does NOT filter — the comment is observational only. The `returns` series passed to all qs.stats calls at L325+ is the SAME series whose len we logged. No bug today. Future-refactor risk only. STATUS: DEFERRED — no current bug.
evidence: metrics.py:324 (computed before any potential filtering). The function has no filtering today; the risk is a future refactor.
fix: Either (a) compute `returns_len_for_log` JUST BEFORE the first qs.stats call (one line move), or (b) add a test assertion `assert returns_len_for_log == len(returns)` at the end of compute_all_metrics. DEFERRED.

title: caplog.at_level(logging.WARNING, logger='quantalyze.analytics') in Bybit tests is broader than needed — may capture WARNINGs from unrelated branches and produce false-positives
summary: The Bybit tests scope caplog at `logger='quantalyze.analytics'` (the parent logger). All warnings emitted under that hierarchy are captured. The test then filters by `'bybit' in r.getMessage().lower()`. This is loose — a future warning from a different module that happens to contain the word 'bybit' would also match. STATUS: APPLIED — Bybit + Binance tests tightened to require exact prefix 'Bybit closed_pnl' / 'Binance futures-income'. exc_info=True propagation also pinned.
evidence: test_exchange.py:2820, 2876 — `logger='quantalyze.analytics'` is the umbrella logger. The substring filter is imprecise.
fix: Tighten the filter to match the EXACT message prefix. APPLIED.
